title: Low-Glycemic Peruvian Snappy Causa-Limena
description: A yeasty, snappy, monsoon Peruvian dish featuring coconut cream, binchotan charcoal, in a low-glycemic-friendly causa limena form, served in a donabe vessel.

ingredients.name: Bingotan charcoal
ingredients.notes: activated charcoal powder for color and flavor

instructions: Combine coconut cream and rice flour in a medium bowl, mixing until smooth.
Add binchotan charcoal and yeast to the coconut cream mixture, stirring until well combined.
Add warm water, egg, salt, and black pepper to the mixture, stirring until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased donabe vessel, cover with a cloth, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on desired size.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten slightly into a disk shape.
Cook the causa limena for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Garnish with chopped scallions and chives, and serve immediately.
